Tests the multiplication of a string and a boolean in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of a type coercion operation in JavaScript. It multiplies the string `"1"` by the boolean `true`, which JavaScript implicitly converts to the number `1`, resulting in the numeric value `1`. The assertion verifies that the expression evaluates to `1`, ensuring that the type coercion behaves as expected. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion rules.
